action.reasoning: Observation: Training crashed with RuntimeError because model was loaded with num_labels=1000 (default from nvidia/mit-b5) instead of 150. The interpolation of [16, 1000, 512, 512] exceeds INT_MAX. Analysis: Must load SegformerForSemanticSegmentation with num_labels=150 via config override. The decode head is randomly initialized anyway (nvidia/mit-b5 is backbone-only), so ignore_mismatched_sizes=True handles the classifier weight resize. Decision: Fix train.py to use SegformerConfig with num_labels=150, train 20 epochs with DataParallel, and submit eval.

Notes:
- UNEXPECTED:	can be ignored when loading from different task/architecture; not ok if you expect identical arch.
- MISSING:	those params were newly initialized because missing from the checkpoint. Consider training on your downstream task.
